design_philosophy.core_loop: Traverse platforming stages, defeat bosses to collect Crests, gain new transformations that unlock previously inaccessible areas, repeat with expanded abilities
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Power acquisition vs. complexity management: each new Crest form provides combat and exploration benefits but increases mechanical complexity; risk-reward in form selection
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Firebrand, a powerful demon seeking revenge and dominion through magical crests; progression through increasingly difficult challenges with meaningful power growth
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Refuses to hand-hold player progression—Metroidvania design forces revisits of old stages with new abilities to discover secrets; multiple endings based on final boss choices; no hand-holding on ability applications; regionally altered difficulty (Somulo boss health doubled in NA version suggests intentional difficulty scaling)

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can revisit stages in any order once accessible, choosing which Crests to pursue first; form selection affects both combat capability and exploration range, allowing some player-driven difficulty adjustment

progression.upgrade_system: Transformation system: collect five Crests (Fire, Earth, Water, Air, Time) to unlock five Gargoyle forms (Ground, Aerial, Tidal, Legendary, Ultimate), each providing specialized combat moves and exploration abilities (wall-clinging, hovering, swimming). Secondary items: talismans improve base abilities, flasks/spell vellums grant consumable magic
progression.unlock_mechanism: Crests are dropped by defeated bosses; new stage areas become accessible only when player obtains the appropriate Crest form that matches environmental requirements (water areas need Tidal form, etc.); completing game with specific ending unlocks Ultimate Gargoyle form and secret Dark Demon boss
replayability_hooks: Three main endings plus secret fourth ending locked behind completing game with best ending and defeating Dark Demon
Multiple stage order possibilities—non-linear boss defeat sequence due to Metroidvania design
Boss Rush category in speedrun community suggests replayability for challenge runs
Hidden areas and items require revisiting stages with new forms
Password system allows save states for alternative playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through stage design—areas blocked by water/lava/air currents that require specific forms to traverse
Boss encounters reveal character relationships (Arma as rival general who grudgingly respects Firebrand)
Crests themselves are narrative devices—collecting them drives both story and gameplay progression
Multiple endings that vary based on player moral choice (kill Phalanx outright vs. seal him vs. transcend power-seeking entirely)
Unused NPC dialogue references spell shop surrounded by walls, suggesting environmental narrative context was more developed

boss_design.philosophy: Bosses serve as gating mechanisms tied to Crest acquisition; each defeated boss drops a Crest, forcing progression through combat. Boss variety requires learning distinct attack patterns. Phalanx final battle has three phases with different confrontations, emphasizing culmination and player choice
notable_bosses: Somulo (Demon Dragon) - opening and recurring boss; altered difficulty between regions (3 hits JP vs 6 hits NA)
Arma - Phalanx's general; recurring rival with respect-based dialogue
Phalanx - final boss with three endings based on player choice; transforms into hideous beast using Crest of Infinity in true ending path
Dark Demon - secret boss unlocked by completing game with best ending and using Ultimate Gargoyle form

faction_indicators: Firebrand vs Phalanx power struggle for control of Demon Realm
Arma's gradual respect for Firebrand shows faction tension dissolving through combat respect
Three ending paths suggest ideological divides: conquest (bad ending anarchy), containment (middle ending), transcendence (good ending refusing power)
danger_signals: Phalanx's complete Crest control at game start represents existential threat to Firebrand's status
Somulo battle immediately after imprisonment establishes captive-fighter scenario
Repeated ambushes by Arma signal unstable political environment
Weakened state after Demon Dragon victory creates vulnerability window Phalanx exploits
environmental_storytelling: Amphitheater imprisonment arena establishes power dynamics before escape
Spell shop surrounded by walls (unused dialogue) suggests controlled, restricted areas
Stage environments become accessible only with specific Crests, mirroring how different factions control different realms
Phalanx's castle as final domain represents seat of power requiring full Crest collection to reach
